sections.content: The Toy Donation Program Feature enables users to contribute toys they no longer need to shelter pets through the website. This feature addresses customer demand for alternative ways to support animals beyond monetary donations, allowing the community to contribute physical items that directly benefit shelter pets awaiting adoption.

have them donated to the pets at the shelters? My kids saw the animals on
your site and they really want to do something nice for them. We were thinking
about gettin

sections.content: This feature builds on the existing donation infrastructure established in Phase 3 (Q1-Q2 2024), which implemented the donation page with payment processing and pet dedication options. The feature aligns with Kim's green color convention for money-related UI elements and must follow Matt's ISO 8601 date format standard for any date-related API responses. Customer feedback shows strong interest in toy donations, gift shop functionality, and targeted giving to specific animals, indicating this feature addresses real user needs.

sections.content: Must comply with Kim's convention for green color on money-related UI elements for any purchase/transaction buttons. Must follow Matt's ISO 8601 date format standard for all API date responses. Must apply Tim's vertical navigation pattern when users are choosing toy categories (not comparing). Must implement Matt's max-height with scroll pattern for toy listing areas to prevent page layout disruption. The feature must allow users to donate physical toys, dedicate toy donations to specific pets, and provide confirmation of successful donations.

sections.content:
1. Create Toy Donation Database Schema
New work to define: Design database tables for toy donations including toy categories, donation records, and pet-toy associations. No existing evidence covers toy donation data structures.

2. Build Toy Category Selection Interface
Implement vertical navigation for toy category selection following Tim's convention for user choice interfaces. Use vertical nav on the left side with category content updating on the right, keeping selection persistent and visible while users browse toy options.

3. Design Toy Listing with Scroll Overflow
Apply Matt's max-height with scroll pattern to toy listing areas. Set max-height to 600px with overflow-y scroll to prevent pushing the page down when there are many toy options available, future-proofing against expanding content lists.

4. Implement Pet Dedication Feature
Reuse the existing pet dedication pattern from the donation page implementation. Include an optional 'dedicate to a pet' toggle that shows a pet selector loading all adoptable pets. Display selected pet cards with standard gender-colored accents (pink for female, blue for male).

5. Style Action Buttons with Green Color
Apply Kim's green color convention to all money-related action buttons including 'Complete Toy Donation' and any purchase-related CTAs. Use green for financial actions to maintain consistency with existing Sponsor Me buttons and Complete Donation buttons, ensuring green pops against neutral backgrounds.

6. Build Toy Donation API Endpoints
New work to define: Create API endpoints for toy donation submission, category retrieval, and donation history. Ensure all date fields return ISO 8601 format per Matt's standard, normalizing dates in the service layer before response.

7. Implement Payment Processing Integration
Leverage existing payment infrastructure from the donation page. Use the same payment form pattern with credit card processing (card number, expiry, CVV) and clean white background for the payment section to minimize visual noise during transaction.

8. Create Confirmation and Receipt Flow
New work to define: Design confirmation page and receipt generation for toy donations. No existing evidence covers toy donation confirmation flows, though monetary donation receipts exist as precedent.

sections.content: Test toy category navigation with vertical layout, ensuring selection persistence. Verify green button styling matches existing financial CTAs like Sponsor Me buttons. Test pet dedication flow reusing adoption chooser endpoint. Validate ISO 8601 date formatting in API responses. Test max-height scroll behavior with large toy catalogs (>20 items). Test payment processing integration with existing credit card infrastructure. Verify mobile responsiveness of vertical navigation pattern. Test edge cases: no available pets for dedication, network failures during payment, empty toy categories.

entity_refs.page_title: Donation Page
entity_refs.item_text: Integration with credit card processing services.

sections.content: No existing toy inventory management system exists, requiring new infrastructure for tracking physical items. Payment processing integration may need modification for non-monetary transactions. Shipping logistics for toy donations are undefined and may require third-party integration. Pet dedication feature may create expectations for real-time pet availability that doesn't match shelter operations. Vertical navigation pattern may need responsive adjustments for mobile devices. Database schema changes require careful migration planning to avoid disrupting existing donation functionality.
